reforms.description: Austria's government has prioritized innovation policy, aiming to streamline the institutional setup and improve the effectiveness of R&D spending, which has increased to around 2.5% of GDP.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Streamlining the institutional framework for innovation is expected to enhance the effectiveness of R&D spending, thereby fostering innovation and productivity growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has reiterated its objective to raise R&D expenditures to 3% of GDP by 2010.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in the governance of innovation policy, aiming to enhance the overall effectiveness of R&D investments in the country.
reforms.source_quote: The priority for innovation policy is welcome but the institutional setup should be streamlined and backed by additional reforms.

reforms.description: The government has initiated a reform to improve human capital by increasing university autonomy and introducing a tutor and mentor system to reduce dropout rates.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing human capital through improved education systems is expected to lead to a more skilled workforce, which is crucial for innovation and productivity growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The new government has established a tutor and mentor system at universities/schools with the intention to reduce the drop-out rate.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it addresses the transition from vocational training to tertiary education, which is essential for improving the quality of the workforce.
reforms.source_quote: Austria's recent university reform to increase universities’ autonomy and gradually introduce a small student fee was an important first step.

reforms.description: The Austrian government initiated university reforms to increase autonomy, introduce performance-based funding, and implement limited student fees, aiming to enhance the quality and relevance of higher education.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing funding and autonomy, the reforms are expected to improve educational quality and align training with labor market needs, thus enhancing human capital.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been initiated and are in the process of being implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the higher education system, aiming to improve the quality and relevance of education, which is crucial for economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: Recent steps include more autonomy for the universities, the introduction of 'performance based funding', and the introduction of student fees on a limited scale.

reforms.description: The Austrian government authorized more Universities of Applied Sciences to offer shorter, practically oriented courses, aiming to better align education with labor market demands.
reforms.component_name: Expansion of Universities of Applied Sciences
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing more relevant and practical education, this reform is expected to enhance employability and meet labor market needs.
reforms.status_evidence: More Universities of Applied Sciences were authorized to select students and offer new courses.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it expands educational options and aligns them with market needs, but it is part of a broader reform package.
reforms.source_quote: More Universities of Applied Sciences were authorized, which can select students and offer shorter and more practically oriented courses.

reforms.description: The ongoing administrative reforms facilitating start-ups and reducing their costs should continue, aiming to create a more favorable environment for entrepreneurial activities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing costs and administrative burdens, the reforms are expected to encourage more individuals to start businesses, thereby increasing innovation and job creation.
reforms.status_evidence: The ongoing administrative reforms facilitating start-ups should continue.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent a significant effort to improve the start-up environment, which can have meaningful impacts on entrepreneurship and innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The ongoing administrative reforms facilitating the start-ups and reducing their cost should continue.

reforms.description: Austria aims to increase its R&D spending to 3% of GDP by 2010, aligning with the Lisbon Agenda to enhance innovation and productivity growth.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing R&D spending is expected to boost total factor productivity, which is crucial for long-term economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has given high priority to increasing R&D spending to meet the Lisbon target.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant commitment to enhancing the innovation landscape in Austria, with potential long-term impacts on productivity and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: A major measure to achieving this is a proposed increase in R&D spending to 3% of GDP by 2010 which would also meet the Lisbon target.

reforms.description: Austria has significantly increased its total R&D spending and aims to raise it to 3% of GDP by 2010, utilizing various policies such as direct subsidies and tax incentives to enhance innovative activity.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased R&D funding is expected to stimulate innovation and productivity growth across sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The government aims to raise R&D spending to 3% of GDP by 2010.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a structural change in R&D funding policy, aiming for a significant increase in public investment in research, which is crucial for long-term economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: Austria has significantly increased its total R&D spending in recent years, and the government aims to further raise it to 3% of GDP by 2010.

reforms.description: The government has introduced a tax credit of 25% for R&D expenditures leading to economically valuable inventions, which increases to 35% for additional R&D expenditures, alongside an 8% research premium for start-ups.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The tax incentives are designed to encourage private sector investment in R&D, thereby fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax treatment of business R&D spending is relatively generous.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform enhances the existing framework for R&D tax incentives, making it more attractive for firms to invest in research activities.
reforms.source_quote: For R&D expenditures which are assessed to be 'leading to an economically valuable invention', a tax credit of 25% can be deducted from pre-tax profits.

reforms.description: The Austrian government plans to establish a new top research centre, the Institute of Science and Technology – Austria (IST-A), to focus on excellent basic research, alongside Graduate Schools for improved research training and clusters of excellence at existing universities.
reforms.component_name: Institute of Science and Technology – Austria
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing the quality of scientific research and training, the initiative aims to foster a skilled workforce that can drive innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government plans to increase the quality of scientific research and its linkage with business via three main policies.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of a top research centre and associated initiatives represents a significant structural change aimed at enhancing Austria's research capabilities and fostering high-tech sector growth.
reforms.source_quote: The Austrian government plans to increase the quality of scientific research and its linkage with business via three main policies: i) the establishment of a new top research centre, called the Institute of Science and Technology – Austria (IST-A), which will focus on excellent basic research.

reforms.description: The Austrian government is rationalizing its fragmented R&D support system by merging promotion agencies and clarifying their roles to improve efficiency and reduce overlap.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By streamlining the R&D support system, the government aims to enhance the effectiveness of funding and reduce administrative burdens, thereby promoting innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The system was rationalized in 2004 by merging some promotion agencies and programmes.
reforms.importance_rationale: The merger of promotion agencies represents a significant overhaul of the R&D support system, aimed at improving coherence and efficiency in innovation policy.
reforms.source_quote: The system was rationalized in 2004 by merging some promotion agencies and programmes, although the system still appears to be complex.

reforms.description: Austria's University Organisation and Studies Act introduced significant reforms to the university system, granting universities more autonomy and establishing a new funding model based on performance agreements and student fees.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased autonomy and performance-based funding are expected to enhance the quality of education and research, thereby improving the skill level of the workforce.
reforms.status_evidence: The new funding system fully entered into force with the budget for the year 2007.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a structural change in the governance and funding of higher education, aiming to improve educational outcomes and research capabilities significantly.
reforms.source_quote: The University Act also introduced a new system of funding that fully entered into force with the budget for the year 2007.

reforms.description: The government is considering allowing universities to select students and set tuition fees, which could enhance the efficiency and attractiveness of tertiary education.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These changes are expected to improve the quality of education and reduce dropout rates, leading to a more skilled workforce.
reforms.status_evidence: The government should consider giving the universities autonomy to set tuition fees and at the same time introduce universal and income-contingent student loan schemes.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, these proposals are still in the discussion phase and would require careful implementation to be effective.
reforms.source_quote: The government should consider giving the universities autonomy to set tuition fees and at the same time introduce universal and income-contingent student loan schemes.

reforms.description: The Austrian government plans to significantly increase public spending in key growth-enhancing areas, including R&D and innovation support, as part of a broader budgetary reform package.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding for R&D and innovation is expected to enhance productivity and foster economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has proposed significant increases in spending on R&D and innovation support.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a structural change in public spending priorities, focusing on R&D and innovation as key drivers of economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The 2007-08 federal budget proposes significant increases in spending on education, R&D and innovation.

reforms.description: The Austrian government has significantly increased its support for innovation to position the country as a technology leader and enhance productivity growth.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased government support for innovation is expected to enhance technological capabilities and productivity, driving economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Government support to innovation was considerably increased.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a structural change in the government's approach to innovation, aiming to elevate Austria's position in global technology and productivity.
reforms.source_quote: Government support to innovation was also considerably increased in order to help Austria become more of a technology-leader rather than a technology follower and to accelerate productivity growth.

reforms.description: Austria has seen a significant increase in business sector R&D activities, realizing the strongest increase among OECD countries between 1995 and 2005, driven by competitiveness gains and reinvestment in research and product innovations.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased R&D activities lead to innovation, enhancing productivity and competitiveness in the business sector.
reforms.status_evidence: Austria realized the strongest increase in business sector’s R&D activities in the OECD.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a structural change in the R&D landscape, significantly enhancing the innovation capacity of the business sector.
reforms.source_quote: Between 1995 and 2005 Austria realized the strongest increase in business sector’s R&D activities in the OECD.

reforms.description: Austria is undergoing a paradigm shift in its innovation policy, moving towards becoming a frontier technology producer rather than merely a technology follower. This involves ambitious initiatives to enhance the science infrastructure, technological capabilities of enterprises, and the human capital of researchers.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By upgrading the science infrastructure and enhancing technological capabilities, the reform is expected to boost productivity and innovation, leading to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has announced ambitious policy initiatives to enhance innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in Austria's approach to innovation, aiming to fundamentally change its position in the global technology landscape, which has broad implications for economic performance.
reforms.source_quote: A 'paradigm shift' about the contribution of innovation and innovation policies to growth is highly topical in Austria.

reforms.description: Austria has introduced the Start-up Promotion Law, which significantly reduced the administrative costs associated with setting up new enterprises, aiming to foster a more conducive environment for startups.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By lowering the barriers to entry for new businesses, the reform is expected to stimulate entrepreneurship and innovation, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Start-up Promotion Law has been enacted and is in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The Start-up Promotion Law represents a significant structural change in the regulatory framework for new businesses, enhancing the startup ecosystem and fostering innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Austria has set a European benchmark by introduction of the Start-up Promotion Law (Neugründungsförderungsge-setz) in 2000 which substantially reduced the administrative cost of setting up new enterprises.

reforms.description: The Austrian government is streamlining its support for innovation by simplifying the tax support system for R&D and ensuring that all R&D subsidy spending programs are evaluated by independent institutions.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By simplifying and evaluating R&D support, the reform aims to enhance the effectiveness of public funding, thereby fostering innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The Austrian research funding system is regularly evaluated and improved, with ongoing adjustments to R&D subsidy programs.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant overhaul of the R&D funding landscape in Austria, aiming for a more coherent and effective innovation support system.
reforms.source_quote: Government support for innovation should be streamlined. The tax support system for R&D should be simplified.

reforms.description: The Austrian government launched the 'Go International' initiative in 2003 to enhance the competitiveness of Austrian businesses through a comprehensive package of measures aimed at promoting exports and foreign direct investment.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The initiative aims to increase the number of exporting companies and enhance business networks, which can lead to higher productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative has been extended until the end of 2007 due to its success.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant structural change in how Austria supports its businesses in international markets, with a broad impact on the economy.
reforms.source_quote: In 2003 an internationalisation initiative 'Go International' was jointly launched... Altogether €50 million of additional finance were earmarked for this initiative.
